    The Resort

    Mt Baw Baw is Melbourne’s closest downhill ski, snowboard, toboggan and snow play resort. Sheltered amongst the beautiful snowgums Mt Baw Baw offers a safe and friendly destination for families, couples and those who wish to learn or improve their skiing and snowboarding skills.

  • Plan a Trip

    • Toboggan Passes
    • Ski & Snowboard Passes

    • School Bookings

    Snow trips should be easy

    Mt Baw Baw makes booking a trip to the snow easy. Use our website to book accommodation, then visit the eStore to book other products. Resort entry, shuttles, lessons, toboggan passes, lift passes, dingo walks and more can be booked online. Still struggling? See our step-by-step guide below.

  • What to Do
    • Events
    • Winter Activities
    • Summer Activities

    Activities & Events

    Mt Baw Baw offers a range of activities and special events through the season to make your snow experience the best it can be. Join us for Christmas in July, ski, snowboard, toboggan or book in a dingo cuddle with our alpine dingoes.

Snow & Lift Report

Home Reports Snow & Lift Report

Updated daily during the snow season

Road Conditions

We also recommend checking VicRoads for alerts.

Resort Entry Road

Chains fitted as directed

The road from the Entry gate to the Resort.

Tourist Rd

The main access road to the Resort. Sealed.

South Face Rd

Open

An alternate access route. Unsealed but wide and less windy. Recommended for those with travel sickness or looking for an easier (but slightly longer) drive.

Snow chains must be carried at all times during the declared snow season (June – October) and fitted if conditions require. Penalties apply for non-compliance.

This report reflects the conditions at the date and time this report was generated. Conditions, lift status and operations may change rapidly, or lifts may close suddenly for safety reasons. “Standby” indicates a lift may be preparing for operation or is on-hold.

Our Snow Guarantee area covers the Toboggan Park, Hut Run to Tower 2 (approx 100m) and the beginner lesson areas from start of declared season in June to the start of September.

Please see detailed status section for full status information and latest updates or subscribe to the Daily Snow Report email.

“Avg. Depth on Runs” refers to depth of snow across all currently open terrain/ski runs.

Traditional Landowners

We acknowledge and respect the Victorian Traditional Owners as the original custodians of Victoria’s land and waters, their unique ability to care for Country and deep spiritual connection to it. We honour Elders past and present whose knowledge and wisdom has ensured the continuation of culture and traditional practices.
